neighborhoods.name: Cork City Centre
neighborhoods.description: Island city center built on the River Lee with the English Market, Patrick Street, and a vibrant pub scene
neighborhoods.character: Island city, market culture, vibrant pub scene

housing.predominantType: Georgian townhouse apartments and modern city-center flats

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Cork. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Douglas
neighborhoods.description: Southern suburb with excellent shopping, parks, and the River Lee walking paths
neighborhoods.character: Suburban residential, shopping access, green spaces
neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Cork's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Ballincollig
neighborhoods.description: Western commuter town with regional park, military heritage, and lower housing costs

housing.predominantType: Estate houses, apartments, and newer developments

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Carrigaline
neighborhoods.description: Southern seaside commuter town with good schools, community facilities, and harbor access

housing.predominantType: Estate houses and modern family homes

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
